mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are below the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 60-day horizons, or the interaction term shows negative predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production below its 6-month moving average indicates supply-side contraction that compresses corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns during tightening cycles, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations remain anchored below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises faster than the monthly inflation index, real interest rates increase, but equity markets price in peak tightening expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The relative pace of rate hikes versus inflation shows no predictive power for 5-day returns, or the signal consistently underperforms during periods of stagflation.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate consumption capacity and corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines show zero or positive correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year sample, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized confirms robust economic expansion, boosting equity risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are flat or negative following GDP prints above 2.5%, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for prior market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent weakness in industrial production and rising unemployment during a recessionary regime signals severe output gaps, forcing immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint supply-labor weakness signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns during NBER-defined recessions, or the mechanism breaks down when fiscal stimulus offsets private sector contraction.
